meta.title: French Revolution
meta.description: Liberty, Equality, Fraternity... and lots of guillotines! 🇫🇷⚡
meta.historical_context: By 1789, France was bankrupt and hungry.

marie-antoinette.name: Marie Antoinette
marie-antoinette.description: The cake-loving queen who allegedly said "Let them eat cake!" 🍰 Known for her extravagant parties and love of fancy dresses while people starved.

robespierre.name: Maximilien Robespierre
robespierre.description: The obsessively clean revolutionary who loved his guillotine a bit too much! 🗡️ Started as a defender of the people, ended as the Terror's chief architect.

louis-xvi.name: Louis XVI
louis-xvi.description: Poor Louis! 👑 Loved making locks more than making decisions. His hobby was locksmithing while his kingdom fell apart!

napoleon-bonaparte.name: Napoleon Bonaparte
napoleon-bonaparte.role: The Little Emperor with Big Dreams
napoleon-bonaparte.description: The short general who conquered most of Europe! ⚡ Rose from the chaos to become Emperor and reshape the world.

jacques-danton.name: Georges Danton
jacques-danton.description: The booming orator who could shake buildings with his voice! 📢 Unfortunately, even his powerful voice couldn't save him from Robespierre.

constitutional-monarchy.title: King Louis Learns to Share! 👑🤝
constitutional-monarchy.description: What if Louis XVI had embraced constitutional monarchy?
constitutional-monarchy.divergence_description: Louis XVI genuinely accepts constitutional limits.
constitutional-monarchy.present_day_status: France leads the European Union as a Constitutional Monarchy. The Bourbons are beloved figureheads. Paris is the capital of peace. 🇫🇷👑

louis-accepts-constitution.title: Louis XVI Accepts Constitution
louis-accepts-constitution.description: Louis becomes a model constitutional king.

robespierre-eternal.title: Robespierre's Eternal Terror! 😱🗡️
robespierre-eternal.divergence_description: Robespierre purges his enemies before Thermidor.
robespierre-eternal.present_day_status: The Republic of Virtue collapsed in 1824. It is remembered as a dark age. Annual "Never Again" parades feature guillotine piñatas. 🎭🗡️

robespierre-survives.title: Robespierre Survives
robespierre-survives.description: The plot fails. The Terror intensifies.

girondist-victory.title: The Girondists Win! Democracy Lite 🕊️🏛️
girondist-victory.description: What if the moderate Girondists defeated the Jacobins?
girondist-victory.present_day_status: The French Federal Republic is the heart of a federal Europe. The Girondist model inspired the world. 🇫🇷🧀

estates-general.title: The Tennis Court Oath (1789)
estates-general.text: Versailles. The King has locked you out. The people are angry. The rain is falling. Where do we meet?

oath.text: To the Tennis Court! Swear a new Constitution!
oath.consequence: The National Assembly is born. The Revolution begins.

compromise.consequence: Louis is touched by your loyalty. He grants a constitution peacefully.

go-home.consequence: The revolution happens without you. You are forgotten.

storming-bastille.title: July 14th (1789)
storming-bastille.text: The Bastille looms above Paris. It holds gunpowder. The mob looks to you for leadership.

assault.consequence: The Bastille falls! The King is terrified.

negotiate.consequence: He opens fire while you talk. The mob massacres everyone. You failed.

flight-varennes.title: The King Escapes (1791)
flight-varennes.text: The King has fled Paris in disguise! He is stopped at Varennes. The crowd surrounds his carriage.

arrest.text: Arrest him! He is a traitor.
arrest.consequence: The Monarchy is dead. The Republic is born.

let-go.text: Let him pass. France needs a King.
let-go.consequence: He returns with an Austrian army. The Revolution is crushed.

kings-trial.title: Trial of Citizen Capet (1793)
kings-trial.text: Louis XVI is on trial. The Jacobins want blood. The Girondins want mercy. How do you vote?

execute.text: Death! The King must die so the nation may live.
execute.consequence: The guillotine falls. Europe declares war.

exile.text: Exile. Do not stain the Republic with blood.
exile.consequence: The King is banished. The Moderates win.

reign-of-terror.title: The Great Terror (1793)
reign-of-terror.text: Robespierre rules. The Committee of Public Safety sees enemies everywhere. Your neighbor has been arrested.

purge.text: Support the purges. Purity is essential.
purge.consequence: You survive, but at what cost?

thermidor.consequence: The coup succeeds! The Terror ends.

napoleon-rises.title: 18 Brumaire (1799)
napoleon-rises.text: General Bonaparte has returned. He plans a coup to restore order. He asks for your support.

emperor.text: Hail Caesar! France needs a strongman.
emperor.consequence: The Empire begins. Glory and war await.

republic.consequence: Napoleon is arrested. The Republic survives (weakly).
